title: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides directory services, authentication, application access management, and identity governance for organizations using Microsoft 365, Azure, and thousands of third-party SaaS applications. The T1 Agentics integration enables SOC teams to query user and group information, manage account states during incidents, review sign-in logs, and enforce security policies.

**Vendor:** Microsoft
**Category:** Identity & Access Management
**Authentication:** OAuth 2.0 (Client Credentials)

## Prerequisites

Before configuring the Microsoft Entra ID integration in T1 Agentics, ensure you have:

- An Azure tenant with Microsoft Entra ID (P1 or P2 recommended for advanced log access)
- An App Registration in the Azure portal with the following:
  - **Application (client) ID**
  - **Directory (tenant) ID**
  - **Client secret** or certificate
- Microsoft Graph API permissions assigned to the app registration:
  - `User.Read.All` (read user profiles)
  - `Group.Read.All` (read group memberships)
  - `AuditLog.Read.All` (read sign-in and audit logs)
  - `User.ReadWrite.All` (for containment actions like disabling accounts)
  - `Directory.ReadWrite.All` (for group management actions)
- Admin consent granted for the above permissions in the Azure portal
- Network access from T1 Agentics to Microsoft Graph API (`https://graph.microsoft.com`)

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics sidebar
2. Search for **Microsoft Entra ID** or browse the **Identity** category
3. Click on the Microsoft Entra ID card and select **Configure**
4. Enter your connection details:
   - **Connection Name**: A descriptive label (e.g., "Entra ID Production")
   - **Tenant ID**: Your Azure AD tenant ID
   - **Client ID**: The Application (client) ID from your app registration
   - **Client Secret**: The client secret value
5. Click **Test Connection** to verify OAuth token acquisition
6. Click **Save** to activate the integration
7. The integration will appear in your **Connect > Active Integrations** list

## Available Actions

| Action | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| List Users | Investigate | Query users with optional filtering using OData syntax |
| Get User | Investigate | Retrieve a specific user's profile by ID or UPN |
| Disable User | Contain | Set accountEnabled to false, blocking all sign-ins |
| Enable User | Correct | Re-enable a previously disabled user account |
| Revoke Sign-In Sessions | Contain | Invalidate all refresh tokens, forcing re-authentication |
| Reset Password | Contain | Reset user password to a temporary value |
| List Groups | Investigate | List all groups with optional filtering |
| Get Group Members | Investigate | List members of a specific group |
| Add User to Group | Generic | Add a user as a member of a group |
| Remove User from Group | Contain | Remove a user from a group |
| List Sign-In Logs | Investigate | Query Azure AD sign-in logs with filters |
| List Audit Logs | Investigate | Query directory audit logs for changes |
| List Risky Users | Investigate | Retrieve users flagged by Identity Protection |
| Get User App Roles | Investigate | List application role assignments for a user |

## Common Use Cases

- **Compromised account response**: Disable the user account, revoke all sign-in sessions, and reset the password in a single automated playbook triggered by a SIEM alert
- **Impossible travel investigation**: Query sign-in logs to investigate alerts about logins from geographically distant locations within a short timeframe
- **Risky user triage**: Pull the list of users flagged by Entra ID Identity Protection and correlate with other threat intelligence sources in T1 Agentics
- **Privilege escalation detection**: Monitor audit logs for unexpected group membership changes, especially to privileged groups like Global Admins or Security Admins
- **Offboarding automation**: When an HR system signals a termination, automatically disable the account, remove group memberships, and revoke all sessions

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| Authentication failures | Verify tenant ID, client ID, and client secret. Ensure the client secret has not expired in the Azure portal |
| Insufficient privileges errors | Confirm that admin consent has been granted for all required API permissions in the app registration |
| Sign-in logs not available | Sign-in log access requires Entra ID P1 or P2 licensing. Verify your tenant has the appropriate license |
| Risky users endpoint returns 403 | The Risky Users API requires Entra ID P2. Upgrade your license or remove this action from playbooks |
| Throttling (429) responses | Microsoft Graph has per-app throttling limits. Reduce concurrent requests and implement retry with backoff |
| User not found by UPN | Ensure the User Principal Name is correct. Guest users may have a different UPN format (e.g., `user_contoso.com#EXT#@tenant.onmicrosoft.com`) |
